rulings.Case Title: JEFFERSON CHRISMAN VS. HOWARD LEE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday September 25, 2018 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT JEFFERSON CHRISMAN, CAROLYN CHRISMAN DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Jefferson Chrisman and Carolyn Chrisman's demurrer to fraud-based causes of action is overruled. All elements of fraud, including causation, are sufficiently alleged. Carolyn Chrisman demurrer to causes of action for assault and battery is sustained without leave to amend. Mr. Lee fails to allege any basis for Mrs. Chrisman's liability under these causes of action. Carolyn Chrisman's demurrer to contract-based and common counts causes of action are sustained with leave to amend. Mr. Lee shall have 10 days to allege facts (not conclusions) showing that 2014 lease was an amendment/modification subject to a guarantee as opposed to being a brand new lease for the premises. If Mr. Lee cannot so allege in good faith, Mr. Lee may amend the complaint asserting these causes of action based only on 2012 lease and guarantee and addressing the statute of limitations issue as to Mrs. Chrisman. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: NOEL MARGARET LAWRENCE VS. BARREL CELLAR LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Barrel Cellar Llc, Jim Howell, Matthew Weese, Sarah Weese'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Against Noel Margaret Lawrence, Lindsay Tusk And Quince Pacific Avenue, Llc
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday September 25, 2018 line 2. Defendants Barrel Cellar Llc, Jim Howell, Matthew Weese, Sarah Weese'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Against Noel Margaret Lawrence, Lindsay Tusk And Quince Pacific Avenue, Llc DENIED. Motion for summary adjudication is DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. There are triable issues of fact as to waiver; whether removed items belonged to the lessor or the lessee and whether those items were permanent or moveable; whether the option to extend the lease was exercised; the extent of damage to the property; whether the non-circumvention agreement covered the purchase the lease and purchase of business only or included the lease of real property. Prevailing parties to prepare an order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) consistent with this tentative rulings. = (501/REQ)
